content: Performance concern: current one-JSONL-file-per-session structure requires scanning all session files and filtering by processed flag. Alternatives under consideration: (1) one file per message with atomic pickup/delete, (2) separate pending/ and done/ directories with file moves on completion, (3) append-only log with offset tracking. No direction chosen yet.

provenance.raw_agent: Some alternative approaches that would scale better: One file per message, Separate pending/ and done/ directories, Append-only log + offset tracking. Which direction were you thinking of taking this?
provenance.raw_user: If they are being processed by another system, isn't that going to cause a performance degradation?
